page: Vultr vbm-8c-132gb is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal NVMe family. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 2288G processor featuring 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz. The system includes 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Storage is provided by a 3840 GB local NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate balanced performance characteristics. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 2024.2, and the multi-core score is 19837.37, both placing in the average tier. Single-threaded gzip compression performance is highly competitive, reaching 36.22 GB/sec to place in the top tier, while single-threaded bzip3 decompression registers at a strong 14.23 GB/sec. Memory performance is mixed; the PassMark memory score is in the top tier at 3594.89, and cached small-block read bandwidth is 1161.67 GB/sec. However, uncached large-block read and write bandwidths are weaker, measuring 29.76 GB/sec and 15.32 GB/sec respectively.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal configuration offers high resource density by combining a dedicated 8-core Intel Xeon processor with a substantial 128 GB memory footprint and a large 3.8 TB local NVMe SSD. This eliminates virtualization overhead, making it a viable option for workloads requiring direct hardware access without the premium of multi-socket enterprise platforms. The server is well-suited for database hosting, memory-intensive applications, static web serving, and local file processing tasks that benefit from fast local NVMe storage and high single-threaded compression speeds.
description: Vultr vbm-8c-132gb is a bare metal server in the Bare Metal NVMe family, powered by an Intel Xeon 2288G processor with 8 physical cores, 16 vCPUs, and a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and a 3840 GB local NVMe SSD. Performance benchmarks show strong single-threaded compression capabilities, with gzip compression reaching 36.22 GB/sec and a top-tier PassMark memory score of 3594.89. While cached memory read bandwidth is high at 1161.67 GB/sec, uncached large-block memory performance is relatively weak. This configuration provides a cost-effective, non-virtualized environment with high storage capacity and memory density, making it suitable for database hosting, static web serving, and memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Vultr vbm-8c-132gb is a bare metal server featuring an 8-core Intel Xeon 2288G CPU, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3840 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-threaded compression and balanced database performance.

tagline: A bare metal server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3.8 TB of high-speed local NVMe storage.

128.0 GB of DDR4 system memory running at 3 GHz
3840 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
